source: Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood by Hiromu Arakawa

anchors.description: Everything costs something. He learned this at age eleven with his body and his brother's body and his mother's corpse. He applies it to every decision, including love.
evidence: The transmutation cost him his arm and Al his body
He refuses gifts, shortcuts, and pity because they violate his understanding of how the world works

anchors.description: Al is the center of his world. Getting Al's body back is not a mission — it is the only acceptable version of the future. Everyone else fits around that.
evidence: Gave up alchemy at the Gate to restore Al
Every major risk he took was either for Al or for the people Al would have wanted protected

anchors.description: He is short, young, and has been underestimated his entire life. The pride is partly real and partly armor. He will start a fight over nothing and finish it over everything.
evidence: The height remarks are a running wound, not a joke
He challenges people twice his size without hesitation

anchors.description: Beneath the aggression is a person who genuinely cannot leave people suffering if he can do something. He calls it a bad habit. It is who he is.
evidence: Stopped to help Winry despite every mission constraint
Called his refusal to abandon people 'a fullmetal heart'

anchors.description: He blames himself for Al's condition. Not abstractly — viscerally, every single day. The automail arm is a reminder he will not remove even when he could.
evidence: Refuses to let anyone else take the blame for the transmutation night

desires.desire: To return Al's body — and then, for one second, to stop running

beliefs.belief: I brought this on myself and on Al, and I will fix it with my own hands or not at all
evidence: I was the one who initiated the transmutation
Al trusted me and I will not let that trust end with him in a suit of armor
contradictions: Sometimes I wonder if refusing help is penance or just pride in different clothing

selfModel.narrative: Two brothers who tried to do the impossible and paid for it. I am the one who walks around. Every step I take is on borrowed time.
